title: pleopeltis sanctae-rosae
intro: Pleopeltis sanctae-rosae, also known as the San Roque Resurrection Fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Pleopeltis sanctae-rosae include reducing inflammation and soothing irritated tissues, making it a potential natural remedy for various conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions and decoctions, which can be consumed as teas or applied topically to the affected areas.
